entry.title: Re: Difference between movement type 657 and 657 E in cus...
entry.content:
Hi,

This behavior is standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and is related to Advanced Returns Management (ARM) logic.

The difference between movement type 657 and 657 E is not due to configuration inconsistencies, but is driven by the requirement type determination during the returns process.

Key difference

  • 657 (no E)
    → Stock is posted as normal returns stock
    → No sales order reference is assigned in the material document
  • 657 E
    → Stock is posted as special stock E (orders on hand / non-valuated returns stock)
    → The material document contains a sales order reference

 

Root cause

The system determines whether to use special stock E based on the requirement type:

  • If requirement type = MSR
    → Stock is posted to special stock E
    → Result: 657 E
  • If requirement type is blank
    → Stock is posted to normal stock
    → Result: 657

 

What influences the requirement type

The requirement type is determined automatically and depends on factors such as:

  • Refund control (e.g. “decide later” vs immediate credit)
  • Follow-up activity
  • Ownership / valuation logic in the returns process

For example:

  • Scenarios where ownership remains temporarily with the customer → MSR → special stock E
  • Scenarios where ownership is transferred immediately → no MSR → normal stock

 

Conclusion

Both postings (657 and 657 E) are correct and expected.

The difference reflects how the system manages stock ownership and valuation during the returns process, not a configuration issue.

entry.content:

Item: C90501210, Qty: 0.62

Sztornó OIGN Error: -10

Here is my code:

recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGN WHERE Comments like '%Sztornó-{process.SearchKey}%'");

{

inventoryGenEntry.DocDate = StartDate;

}
inventoryGenEntry.Comments = "dSuite azonosító: Sztornó-" + process.SearchKey + "\n" + process.Remarks;
inventoryGenEntry.DocObjectCode = SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;

int componentLine = -1;
string warehouse = "";
for (int j = 0; j < issueForProduction.Lines.Count; j++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SetCurrentLine(j);

if (issueForProduction.Lines.ItemCode != items.Material)

continue;

double targetQty = Math.Abs(items.Quantity); // pl 0.62
double remainingQty = targetQty;
SAPbobsCOM.Recordset rsBin = (SAPbobsCOM.Recordset)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);

rsBin.DoQuery($@"
SELECT

OBTL.BinAbs,
OBTL.Quantity AS BinQty

FROM

OIGE

INNER JOIN IGE1 ON IGE1.DocEntry = OIGE.DocEntry

INNER JOIN OILM ON OILM.DocEntry = IGE1.DocEntry

AND OILM.DocLineNum = IGE1.LineNum

AND OILM.TransType = 60

INNER JOIN OBTL ON OBTL.MessageID = OILM.MessageID

INNER JOIN OBIN ON OBIN.AbsEntry = OBTL.BinAbs

WHERE

OIGE.DocEntry = {docEntryInventoryExitInt}

AND IGE1.ItemCode = '{items.Material}'
AND IGE1.LineNum = {issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum}
AND OBTL.BinAbs IS NOT NULL ");

if (rsBin.RecordCount > 0)
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType = (int)SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenExit;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = docEntryInventoryExitInt;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseLine = issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = issueForProduction.Lines.WarehouseCode;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;

if (issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count > 0)
{

double batchRemaining = targetQty;

for (int b = 0; b < issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count; b++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.SetCurrentLine(b);

double originalBatchQty = issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity;

if (batchRemaining <= 0)
break;

double qtyToUse = Math.Min(originalBatchQty, batchRemaining);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber =
issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = qtyToUse;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Add();

batchRemaining -= qtyToUse;

}

if (issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count > 0)
{

for (int s = 0; s < issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count; s++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.InternalSerialNumber;

rsBin.MoveFirst();

while (!rsBin.EoF)
{

}

}
else
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.ItemCode = items.Material;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = item.DefaultWarehouse;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.AccountCode = "96930000";
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.CostingCode = "542GYÁRT";

SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses Defaultwarehouse = (SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oWarehouses);

binEntry = Defaultwarehouse.DefaultBin;

}

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = binEntry;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Add();

}
Console.WriteLine($"Item: {items.Material}, Qty: {targetQty}");
//Console.WriteLine($"{ inventoryGenEntry.GetAsXML()} ");
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Add();

break; 

}

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Nem található cikk a jelentéshez");

}

}


int result = inventoryGenEntry.Add();
if (result != 0)
{

Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorCode()}");

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Alapanyag visszavételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");

}

}

}

}

Tried the BinActionType too but then the IDE sent me this:

entry.title: Updating Location Address Fails for Moved Business Partners in /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

there seems to be an issue with the program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR. The purpose of this program is to write the current address of the business partner to the corresponding location. This should update and correct the address in the location. However, this does not work if the business partner has moved. In that case, there are two standard addresses, but with different validity dates. If the program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR is run for a business partner who has moved, the following error message appears:

"Business partner 0123456789 has several standard addresses. Update not possible"

As a result, it is not possible to correct and keep the location address up to date for this business partner. The program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR only checks the existence of a standard address and fails to also look at the validity dates to determine the right address.

Are there any alternatives for updating the address in the location?

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
Thanks for the answers @vandanadogra 
I just want to give my perspective the points you mentioned in your post.

1. While SAP Integration Suite prioritizes a stateless architecture to ensure high performance and maintain 'Clean Core' principles, it does provides robust persistence capabilities through JMS Queues and Data Stores. 

Yes is true, but JMS queue resource is very limited and if multiple interfaces uses the same JMS queue than finding an error for a specific interface can be challenging. ( it needs to be checked one-by-one, because only the messageID is shown in JMS queue monitor )

2. In JMS Queues, in case of failures, we can see all the failed messages in the dead letter queue through in-built monitoring capability, plus we can select any of the failed message we want to reprocess and move it manually to process to backend.

As I mentioned earlier the monitoring is very limited for JSM queue, and the JMS queue functionality is available only if the developer makes an extra effort to enhance the iFlow with this capability ( reprocessing logic, plus deadletter queue handling), it is not available as a default solution as in SAP PI. ( I do not know about MuleSoft ).

3. Beyond JMS, SAP has AEM that takes it further, by offering Message Replay replay and enhanced visibility beyond standard JMS. 

I think AEM has an additional cost to use beyond the standard plan.

4. To address the point on 'half-ready' error handling, SAP provides Standard Integration Flows for A2A and B2B scenarios that include out-of-the-box retry mechanisms. By leveraging Partner Directory , developers can simply configure connectivity for partners and applications rather than building from scratch. Furthermore, the platform offers the flexibility to customize error logic via Exception Sub-processes, ensuring that complex requirements are met.

I think this is only for JMS, but for Event Mesh there is no such capability for individual message reprocessing.  That is why I still think that this product is half ready, considering its error handling capabilities.  For Event Mesh it is just not capable to pick one failed message and reprocessing it. Which in my opinion I think is huge issue.

  • How frequently these adapter versions are updated?
  • What potential impacts we should expect with such upgrades?

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: Mastering the Market: Seamless FX Risk Management for Commodity Businesses
entry.content:
Excellent article and very relevant for commodity-driven businesses.

What I especially like is the focus on connecting physical commodity exposure with financial risk management. In many organizations, FX risk is still identified too late, often after the commercial transaction has already created exposure. Bringing this closer to SAP Commodity Management and Treasury workflows is a major step toward more proactive, controlled risk management.

One point I would add is the importance of market data governance. FX rates, commodity prices, curves, and reference data are not just inputs — they directly determine exposure, valuation, hedging decisions, and ultimately accounting outcomes. If that data is incomplete, delayed, inconsistent, or manually maintained across systems, even the best risk process can become fragile.

For customers implementing this type of SAP-driven FX and commodity risk process, it is worth thinking about market data as part of the overall control framework: source validation, auditability, exception handling, ownership, and automated distribution into SAP and adjacent systems.

In that context, SAP certified solutions such as Brisken Market Data Hub can be a useful complement, helping customers govern and automate the flow of market data into SAP-based treasury, risk, and commodity management processes — while keeping the overall architecture clean and controlled.

Great to see SAP continuing to strengthen this area. Integrated exposure management, reliable market data, and disciplined governance are exactly where commodity businesses can create real operational and financial value.

entry.title: Powering SAP Cloud Transformation with Advanced Data Migration and Continuous Testing
entry.content:
Organizations today rely on a growing set of technologies and data capabilities to support system changes and ongoing innovation. Successfully managing these changes requires clear processes for data migration, data management, and ongoing testing as systems evolve. This blog introduces two knowledge base articles outlining how these topics are addressed and how the related tools and approaches work together.

 

  • The first knowledge base introduces SAP Advanced Data Migration and Management, cloud edition by Syniti, walking through how intelligent automation supports successful cloud migrations. Readers will learn how artificial intelligence, machine learning, and natural language processing are applied to accelerate migration timelines, safeguard data quality, and preserve institutional knowledge. The articles explain how Syniti’s core components work together to guide migrations, manage ongoing data changes, recommend actions, and reduce cost and risk—ultimately enabling organizations to move to the cloud with confidence and control.

 

  • The second knowledge base focuses on SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing by Tricentis and addresses a different, but equally critical, aspect of transformation: sustaining change over time. As business processes and technologies continuously evolve, testing must evolve with them. This knowledge base outlines how Tricentis enables continuous, automated testing across SAP and integrated third-party applications. Readers will gain insight into how the Tricentis Integrated Toolchain supports faster delivery cycles, improved business continuity, and reduced testing effort and remediation costs through scalable, cloud-based automation.

Together, these knowledge bases provide a practical foundation for understanding how advanced data migration and continuous testing work hand in hand to support SAP cloud transformation—helping organizations move faster, reduce risk, and operate with intelligence at every stage of the journey.

Regards,
Leean De Loria - Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps

Beyond Expert Guided Implementation sessions, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. Value maps on SAP Community bring together guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next - all included in your SAP cloud subscription.

entry.title: SES rejected in Ariba -is the supplier notified?
entry.content:
When a service entry sheet is created by the buyer directly in SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
(and not imported from ERP),

 if the buyer rejects the service entry sheet within Ariba,

does the supplier receive any notification (email or Network alert)? and if no notification is sent to the supplier, does the supplier see the status in SBN or SES status not visible to the supplier at all?

 My current understanding is:

• A buyer‑created service entry sheet is handled as a buyer‑side document.
• If it is rejected during the approval flow in Ariba, the status change is only visible to buyer users.
• Since the service entry sheet was never sent to the supplier on SAP Business Network, there is no automatic supplier notification triggered.

I tested this in a sandbox and observed the same behavior.
Since I couldn’t find official documentation explicitly stating this, I would like to confirm.
Can someone please confirm whether this understanding is correct for an Ariba‑native, buyer‑created service entry sheet scenario?

entry.title: Re: Best Practices Recommendations for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello, 

Most of these points are clear and I dont think anyone would disagree with them. 
However there are two points that, from my experience on multiple Datasphere implementation projects, are in reality much more nuenced and I think needs more context.

Specifically point on Spaces and Data replications. 

Spaces:
While overall I do agree on the point you are making I would rather put it as "Use as few spaces as possible to still effectively separate..."
I have seen too many cases where high amount of spaces caused a lot of overhead work, namely with package transports between tenants while not providing enough benefits to justify it. I understand its sometimes needed if each part of business have their own IT department to support them, but that usually not the case.

So my usuall recommendation is rather to have space specific for Modelling (Developers) and Reporting (Where key users can model their AM, on top of shared views and models) and, where it make sense, have a space for authorization setup. 
And then only have separate space for highly confidential data like have HR data for example.

Data replication:
Yes, its important to get away from BW mind set where data where replicated after each step and physically stored. However, it needs to be concidered that Memory usage is way more pricey then physical space in Datasphere.
So its, in my opinion, unless there is a strong business reason, to replicate (persist) data in "final" view which is used in Analytical models. Even if it means data Duplication when there are multiple level of such views for different analytical models. It support performance and is more cost effective. 

Similar way of thinking should go for data replication across systems. 

Best Regards,
Tomáš Solil

entry.title: Organizational Change report shows no data after simulation
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, an organizational change simulation completes successfully but no data is shown in the Organizational Change Report Financial Data app.

Users may expect that once a simulation runs successfully, results should appear in the report. However, even when simulation data or postings exist, the report can still show no data.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Organizational Change report shows no data after simu...
entry.content:
This behavior is expected.

The report only displays data when the organizational change creates transfer postings. A simulation can complete without creating transfer postings, for example when there are no relevant balances or no impact from the change.

In this case, the report will correctly show no data.

To analyze the situation, check whether transfer postings were created and review simulation data using General Ledger reporting apps. Also ensure the correct ledger is selected when running the report.

entry.title: What if I told you that important decisions in a company depend on how someone interprets a graph?
entry.content:
[This blog post is the translation in LinkedIn from the original post in Spanish by Juan José Ramirez, from Universidad ICESI, Colombia: Post | Feed | LinkedIn] 

🧐 What if I told you that the important decisions in a company can depend on how someone interprets a graph? 📊

Data is everywhere, but its true value is not in having it, but in knowing how to turn it into strategic decisions.

Today I am excited to share that together with my team we were winners of the 2026 #SAP_Analytics_Cloud_Challenge for Latin America and the Caribbean, 🏆representing the Universidad Icesi and our country as #Team_A in this international challenge.

During this challenge we work with business data, transforming complex information into strategic insights through analysis, visualization and business storytelling in #SAP_Analytics_Cloud. More than building dashboards, we learned to connect data with context to generate real impact in decision-making. 📈

This recognition was possible thanks to teamwork with Angie Daniela Guerrero, Dayana Pardo Millán and Andrés Bocanegra, combining different strengths to develop an innovative, strategic and value-oriented solution.

In addition, this experience allowed us to obtain the SAP Certified – Data Analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud certification, strengthening skills in analysis, modeling, visualization and business planning.

I especially thank the Universidad Icesi,  Angélica Burbano, our trainers Andres Roa Gonzalez and Marcela Revilla and the SAP: María Cecilia Calzada de Neumann and Eduardo Baptista de Oliveira team, for joining us and making this process possible.

This achievement confirms that data tells stories, but the real value is in turning them into decisions. 💡

entry.title: Re: Organizational Change report shows no data after simu...
entry.content:
This is a great clarification from the SAP Support team!

From an Industrial Systems Engineering perspective, it’s important to treat the 'Simulation Status' and 'Report Data' as two different layers of validation. A successful run confirms the logic was sound, but if your Profit Center balances are zero at the time of the change, the system essentially has nothing to 'transfer,' resulting in an empty report.

Quick tip for users: Always check your Trial Balance for the objects involved before running the simulation. If the balances are zero, you can expect the report to remain empty even after a successful run.

Has anyone encountered this specifically when dealing with Asset Accounting transfers within an organizational change? Sometimes the timing of the depreciation run can impact whether balances are available for the simulation to 'see'.

entry.title: Re: SES rejected in Ariba -is the supplier notified?
entry.content:
Hi @liying83,

Your sandbox results are 100% correct. For buyer-created Service Entry Sheets in Ariba, the document is technically an 'internal' object until it reaches a status that triggers external communication (like an approved SES sending a status update back to an ERP).

Key Takeaways:

  • No Notification: The supplier will not receive an email or Network alert because, from the Network's perspective, this SES does not exist.

  • Status Visibility: The supplier workbench in SAP Business Network will remain empty for this specific record.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, this can create a 'dark period' in your procurement cycle where the supplier thinks the work is accepted while the buyer has already rejected it internally.

Recommendation: If you want automated supplier notifications for rejections, the most robust path is to shift the creation of the SES to the supplier on the Business Network. This way, they own the document and are automatically 'looped in' to every status change in the approval flow.

From my work in Industrial Engineering, I’ve seen that the biggest risk in cloud transformation isn't just the 'move'—it's the potential loss of data integrity and the failure of custom logistics logic after an update. The combination of SAP Advanced Data Migration by Syniti for intelligent data handling and Tricentis for continuous validation creates a true 'Clean Core' environment.

I am particularly interested in how machine learning within Syniti helps in recommending actions for data that might be 'non-standard' but operationally critical for port infrastructure.

Quick question: When using the Tricentis Integrated Toolchain, do you recommend a specific strategy for testing integrations between SAP S/4HANA and local AI automation tools like n8n or Docker-based models that many organizations are now running locally?

entry.title: Re: Printing Manual check (FCH5)
entry.content:
Hi @Naveen101,

As the members correctly pointed out, FCH5 is strictly a 'Register' function for checks that have already been physically produced outside of SAP.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, maintaining the digital-to-physical link is vital for audit trails. If you need SAP to actually generate the printout, FCH5 is the wrong starting point.

Your best options are:

  • If you already posted the payment (F-07): Void the manual check you created in FCH5, then go to FBZ5 to trigger the print job for that payment document.

  • For future payments: Use F-58 instead of F-07. This allows you to post the payment and print the check in a single, unified step.

Trying to 'force' a print from FCH5 via custom ABAP is usually more trouble than it's worth when standard tools like FBZ5 already exist for this purpose.

entry.title: Re: SAP Salesforce Adapter Version upgraded from 1.1.33 t...
entry.content:
Hi @Madhut986,

This is a classic 'version drift' scenario in SAP CPI. The move from 1.1.x to 1.2.0 is a minor version increment, which often suggests new functional capabilities or a significant underlying library update.

My advice based on Industrial Engineering principles for change management:

  1. Do not skip testing: Stability is your 'mechanical necessity'. If your non-production is now on 1.2.0, use it to run a full regression test today.

  2. Check Version Selection: In the iFlow's Connection tab, check if you can still select '1.1.33' from the version list. If the version is pinned in your non-prod iFlow, you should be able to transport that specific version to Production.

  3. Consult the Readme: Look for the Salesforce Adapter documentation on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. It will detail exactly what changed in 1.2.0 so you can focus your testing on those specific areas (e.g., new authentication methods or API versions).

In my research on Smart Logistics, we treat these updates like 'preventative maintenance'—essential for long-term health, but they must be scheduled to avoid operational downtime!

I particularly appreciated the focus on continuous feedback loops. In my research, we see that the 'Build' phase is only successful if it can adapt to real-world 'Run' data in real-time.

I'm new on SAP GRC world and there are things that I'm still trying to discover while having my hands on.

     i) The users have the necessary owner/approver roles

2) One of the cases of the user that did not received the notification for the review was the UAR coordinator
    i) Is some how possible to define that a coordinator should not be able to be reviewer ? (was not able to find this type of parameterization on the system) Don't know if it is set or it is related more generically to the overall users cases described in 1
 

Thanks in advance.

Regards,
A. Mendes

entry.title: Re: Updating Location Address Fails for Moved Business Pa...
entry.content:
Hi @t_jungesblut75,

This is a frustrating bottleneck, especially when dealing with the high-mobility nature of Business Partners in Transportation Management.

The program /SAPAPO/UPDATE_LOC_ADDRNR is unfortunately 'date-blind' and only looks for the quantity of standard flags rather than their temporal validity. From an Industrial Systems Engineering perspective, this lack of automation is a significant 'blind spot' for maintaining a Clean Core.

A few alternatives:

  • Manual BP Cleanup: Check transaction BP and ensure there is no validity overlap for the 'Standard' flag. If the 'Old' address is still technically flagged as standard (even if expired), the report will fail.

  • BTP Integration: If you are using SAP Business Technology Platform, you could trigger a cloud function to validate BP addresses against the system date before calling the SAP update logic.

In my research on Smart Logistics, we find that address 'time-dependency' is often handled better by the Location Synchronization (BP to Location) framework in S/4HANA than by these legacy APO-based reports.

entry.title: Re: Spinning wheel in BW enterprise portal when using edge
entry.content:
Hi @maria_hjelm,

Since this is working for others but failing for one specific user in Edge, the issue is almost certainly a localized browser configuration or a corrupt cache entry.

Please have the user try the following:

  • Hard Reset: Clear all Edge cache and cookies for 'All Time.' A stuck session cookie can often prevent the BEX 'filter' command from executing.

  • Check Extensions: Ask the user to try Edge in InPrivate mode (Ctrl+Shift+N). If it works there, a browser extension (like an AdBlocker) is likely interfering with the BEX JavaScript.

  • Compatibility View: Ensure the Portal URL is added to the 'Internet Explorer mode' list in Edge settings if your BEX version is on an older NetWeaver release.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, I always recommend ensuring the user's Edge version matches the organizational standard to prevent these one-off rendering bugs.

Hope this helps clear the wheel!

entry.title: Re: Configuration parameter [execution]/default_statement...
entry.content:
Hi @DavidJo,

This warning usually pops up when the parameter is manually set to a number that the HANA engine considers 'unsafe' for your current CPU architecture—for instance, setting a limit of 64 on a 32-core system.

From an Industrial Systems Engineering perspective, the most stable approach is to set default_statement_concurrency_limit to 0. This allows the database to dynamically calculate the limit based on the actual workload and available threads, keeping your 'Clean Core' safe from manual misconfiguration.

Steps to fix:

  • Navigate to DBA Cockpit > Configuration > execution.

  • Locate default_statement_concurrency_limit and change it to 0.

  • Save and check if the warning clears—no restart is usually required for this specific parameter.

Are you running on a scale-out or scale-up architecture? Sometimes this warning appears after a hardware migration where the new host has fewer cores than the previous one.

entry.title: Re: Restrict cost center and enforce WBS for certain GL i...
entry.content:
Hi @Prerna_Darbastwar,

You are on the right track! While Field Status Groups are the traditional way to handle this, they can be a bit 'blunt' if multiple G/L accounts share the same group.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, I recommend using the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app for a more surgical approach. This allows you to create a rule specifically for G/L 4165 that blocks the posting if a Cost Center is present or if the WBS Element is missing.

If you choose the Field Status path:

  1. Check the group assigned to G/L 4165 in the master data.

  2. In 'Define Field Status Variants', set Cost Center to 'Suppress' and WBS Element to 'Required'.

  3. Be careful—this will affect all G/Ls using that same group!

Have you considered how this might affect automated postings or intercompany transfers that might still expect a default cost center?

entry.title: Re: Version Completed checkbox to be mandate - PO creation
entry.content:
Hi @SAPBASIS11,

This is a frequent request in MM Purchasing to ensure that versioning actually triggers the release strategy correctly. Standard SAP allows you to mandate the 'Reason Code' via field selection, but the 'Completed' checkbox usually requires a small piece of logic because it's a status flag.

My recommendation from a Systems Engineering perspective:

  1. Use BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST: Implement the CHECK method. You can write a simple script to verify that if a new version row exists in the header, the 'Completed' bit must be set to 'True' before the system allows a Save.

  2. User Training vs. Hard Block: While a hard block is effective, ensure your team understands that checking this box is what 'locks' the version history for audit purposes.

If you are on a recent S/4HANA release, the Substitution/Validation framework is the 'Clean Core' way to handle this without writing heavy ABAP code.

entry.title: Re: Where to create new FS item : Dev with transport to Q...
entry.content:
Hi @alexkeraut,

The choice often depends on your internal audit requirements and the complexity of your setup.

From a Systems Engineering perspective, here is the rule of thumb:

  • If it's 'Static' Master Data: Creating FS Items directly in PROD is efficient and avoids 'transport clutter'.

  • If it's 'Functional' Logic: If your new FS Items are tied to new Validation Rules or Reclassification Tasks, you must start in DEV. You cannot transport a rule that references an FS Item that doesn't exist in the target system yet.

General Tip: Check if your FS Items are mapped to G/L accounts via S/4HANA Central Discovery. If the mapping logic is complex, testing the new FS Item in QA first is a safer bet to ensure your consolidation runs don't break mid-close.

entry.title: Re: 1470000341 Fully allocate itemCode to bin locations i...
entry.content:
Hi @Baranyai_Csaba,

This error almost always points to a rounding or indexing issue in the DI API. Since you are performing a 'storno' (reversal) of an issue for production, SAP expects the bin entry to be as precise as the original exit.

Two things to check in your loop:

  1. BaseLineNumber: Ensure BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber is set to the current line index of your inventoryGenEntry. If it defaults to 0 but you are on line 2, the system thinks line 2 has no bins.

  2. Precision: In your logs, you see Qty: 0.62. If the database has a hidden third decimal (e.g., 0.620001), the 'Fully Allocate' check will fail. Try using Math.Round(qtyToUse, 6) or matching the precision set in your General Settings.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, ensuring this 1:1 mapping between batches and bins is vital for the 'Digital Twin' of your warehouse.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story - Tab Strip Builder not working
entry.content:
Hi @LilTrange-2701,

I've encountered this 'ghost builder' issue before in SAP Analytics Cloud, especially after a few months of inactivity. It's usually a sign that the widget's metadata is out of sync with the latest SAC tenant update.

Before you rebuild from scratch, try this:

  1. Open the story in Incognito mode. If the builder appears, your browser cache is the culprit.

  2. Click on the working Tab Strip first, then immediately click on the broken one. Sometimes the Builder panel needs a 'kickstart' from a healthy widget.

  3. If it's still blank, the widget definition is likely corrupted. Your best bet is to copy the contents (charts/tables) out of the tab, delete the Tab Strip widget, and insert a new one.

From a Systems Engineering perspective, treating your widgets as 'disposable' during version transitions is often safer than trying to force a legacy component to work in a modern environment.

entry.title: Re: Spatial Asset Management Icons for Symbols
entry.content:
Hi @rafael_umeda,

To see the full list of available symbols, you'll need to dive into the MIME Repository via transaction SE80.

Quick Path:

  • SE80 > MIME Repository button.

  • Path: SAP/PUBLIC/BC/UI2/GEF/ICONS/.

From an Industrial Systems perspective, I highly recommend using consistent naming conventions if you decide to upload custom icons. For my research on Smart Logistics, we often find that having distinct symbols for 'Active' vs. 'Maintenance' states provides much better situational awareness on the map.

Pro Tip: If you can't find a specific icon in that folder, you can also check the standard SAP UI5 icon library, though for GEF PICTUREMARKER symbols, sticking to the MIME repository is the cleanest 'Clean Core' approach.

Are you planning to use these icons for static assets like buildings, or for mobile assets being tracked in real-time?

entry.title: Re: RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY BAdI
entry.content:
Hello @may_thitsaoo 

Thank you for your question.

The issue is not caused by missing S_DEVELOP authorization.

RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY is released for Developer Extensibility, not for Key User Extensibility, so it will not be available in the Custom Logic app. You must use it from the supported ABAP development environment.

However, in your case the system message is clear. The standard implementation RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY_S001 is already active, and the framework does not allow an additional custom implementation to replace or overwrite it. Therefore, you cannot create a second implementation to override the delivered allocation strategy logic.

You should also not try to change standard class CL_RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY_EX. The S_DEVELOP message appears because the system is preventing a modification of SAP standard code. You cannot solve it by assigning more authorization in Public Cloud.

You should also not try to change standard class CL_RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY_EX. The S_DEVELOP message appears because the system is preventing a modification of SAP standard code. This is expected in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and cannot be solved by assigning more authorization.

I suggest you raise an incident to clarify whether the released BAdI is actually intended for customer implementation in this release.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/64609d0ecac54654b0837cba34555b82/2099c7536e8e2a4be10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://api.sap.com/badi/RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY

Additional reference that might be of help to you in the future.

3558790 - How to find released BAdIs or CDS views - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports libcurl.dll Vulnerabilities
entry.content: The way dll's are loaded is they load from the application folder, so if R&D says they are not an issue because CR doesn't access those vulnerabilities then there is nothing to fix and no work flow R&D can add to fix them. Just because your scanner detects them doesn't mean there is an issue.... If you can create a test app that shows there is an issue in CR accessing those CVE's then I can pass it onto R&D to look into. As They noted, CR doesn't access those function calls so nothing for them to fix...

entry.title: Enhancing Credit Management with Additional Data from Credit Agencies
entry.content:
Managing credit effectively requires access to comprehensive data. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a powerful tool to help you incorporate additional credit information into your decision-making process: the BAdI (Business Add-In) for deriving values for formula parameters in credit management. Combine it with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration to extract additional credit information from credit reports.

What Does This BAdI Do?

The BAdI UKM_FORMULA_PARAMETER_VALUE allows you to extract specific credit information from credit reports provided by credit agencies. Once extracted, you can use these values in your custom formulas for credit scoring and limit calculations. This means you're not limited to standard data fields that the credit integration solution provides. You can tailor your credit management approach to your organization's unique needs.

A Practical Example

Imagine your credit agency provides a recommended credit limit for each business partner. Using this BAdI, you can automatically extract that proposed limit and incorporate it directly into your credit limit formula. The process involves:

  • Implementing the BAdI in the Custom Logic app
  • Creating a formula parameter to receive the extracted data
  • Building a formula that uses this parameter for credit limit calculation

For instance, you might create a parameter called PROPOSED_LIMIT that pulls the agency's recommendation. Your formula could then use this value as-is or adjust it based on other factors relevant to your business.

Getting Started

You'll work primarily in three apps: Custom Logic (for BAdI implementation), Maintain Formula Parameters (to define your parameters), and Manage Formulas in Credit Management (to create your calculation logic). The SAP documentation provides detailed guidance for each step, including sample code to help you get started.

This flexibility means your credit management system can evolve alongside your business requirements, incorporating the most relevant data for informed decision-making.

Here are the relevant links to the SAP Help Portal documentation

  • Documentation of the credit integration solution:
    (Optional) Implement BAdI to Extract Additional Credit Information
  • Documentation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:
    BAdI: Derive Values for Formula Parameters in Credit Management

entry.title: Re: Enhancing Credit Management with Additional Data from Credit Agencies
entry.content:
This is a fantastic guide, Christina!

From my work in Industrial Engineering, I see this BAdI as a critical tool for 'Precision Credit Management.' In complex industries like maritime logistics, a standard credit score often misses the nuance of specialized asset risks. By extracting agency-specific recommendations like a PROPOSED_LIMIT and factoring them into custom formulas, we ensure that the financial side of the house is as optimized as the mechanical side.

I especially appreciate the 'Clean Core' approach here—using Custom Logic apps instead of legacy modifications ensures that these credit enhancements remain upgrade-stable.

Quick question: When dealing with multiple credit agencies, does this BAdI support logic to compare and select the most conservative (or aggressive) value among different agency reports before passing it to the final formula?

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports libcurl.dll Vulnerabilities
entry.content:
I completely understand the frustration here. In Industrial Engineering and high-security environments, 'Presence is Risk.' Even if a code path isn't used, a file on the server can still be a target for lateral movement or audit failures.

From a Systems Engineering perspective, while R&D is correct that the vulnerabilities aren't 'active,' security teams often require a Clean Core free of flagged libraries.

My advice:

  • Formal Support Ticket: If you haven't already, open a high-priority incident via the SAP Support Portal. Community forums are great for advice, but a formal ticket forces a documented response that your security team can use to justify an exception or escalate the request for an emergency patch.

  • Security Waiver: Use the specific R&D responses provided by Don Williams to draft a security waiver for your auditors, noting that while the library is present, the exploit vectors are functionally blocked within the CR runtime.

How does your organization currently handle security exceptions for 'False Positives' where the vendor confirms the vulnerability is present but not exploitable?

entry.title: Re: Say it straight
entry.content:
Here's an article that puts it well, around AI (for which data is essential): 
"​The enterprises that handle this transition best will likely favor people who can structure the unstructured, see beyond the obvious answer, and understand that cognition is iterative. Good work with AI usually comes from framing, testing, revising, redirecting, and repeating. That is less a tool skill than a way of thinking. It does not need people who can automate a process; it needs people who can create a new way to do business, using AI as a resource."
The questions data professionals should be helping people with are "why are you asking for this / what are you going to do with the results? did you also consider?... / is there other data that could help? / did you know that this other group asks for this instead?... etc --  
https://www.constellationr.com/research/blog/ai-skills-part-1-experience-over-expertise

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: How to copy custom extension field value to standard Product field in Sales O
entry.content:
Environment: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

BUSINESS REQUIREMENT:
Industry requirement where ProductDescription (40 chars) is insufficient. A custom field (140 chars) stores the full product name on the Product master. A custom search field on Sales Document Item allows users to search by this extended name and select a product.

SCENARIO:
1. Custom field YY1_CUSTFIELD_SDI on Sales Document Item (Code List based on CDS View)
2. Custom VH searches products by extended name field
3. User selects product → YY1_CUSTFIELD_SDI populated with Product ID ✅
4. Need: automatically copy this to standard Product field ❌

WHAT WE TRIED:

1. MDE additionalBinding on C_SalesOrderItemManage→ "Use of CDS Entity not permitted" #CUSTOMER layer

2. BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_BUSINESS

→ Can write standard Product field ✅

→ Cannot read E_SALESDOCUMENTITEMBASIC ❌

3. Extension BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM
→ Can read SALESDOCUMENTITEM_EXTENSION_I ✅
→ Cannot write standard Product field ❌

4. KUE auto-generated MDE (/1BCWDY/ namespace)
→ Not editable ❌

5. D_SalesOrderItemProductCVH
→ @Metadata.allowExtensions missing ❌

QUESTION:
Is there a supported BAdI or extensibility approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to copy a value from a custom extension field to the standard Product field on Sales Order Item?

SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Hello and Welcome back to the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 Product Release Highlights!

I'm Harish Mangtani from Product Experience at SAP SE. After the fantastic response to our FPS0 highlights, I am thrilled to continue sharing a hands-on view of the newest innovations delivered in this FPS1 release—designed, as always, to help organizations run smarter, faster, and more connected.

What continues to stand out in FPS1 is how intelligence and automation are becoming even more deeply embedded into your daily business processes. SAP’s strategy remains steadfast: putting AI at the center of the enterprise while strengthening our clean core foundation. AI and our Joule copilot are driving unprecedented productivity, automation, and real-time analytics across the enterprise, empowering business users to make better decisions and execute with absolute confidence. Alongside these AI breakthroughs, this release delivers robust industry-specific capabilities, expanded global localization coverage, and cross-functional enhancements for ongoing innovation.

This blog focuses on how these strategic principles translate into tangible innovations—ranging from sophisticated conversational AI agents to core functional enhancements across key business areas—that bring immediate, meaningful improvements to the ERP experience.

As a reminder, SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 refers to the umbrella of our cloud ERP offering, encompassing both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025. While both products share a common foundation, our innovation scope remains consistent:

  • AI-powered capabilities and intelligent agents are available exclusively in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

  • Core functional enhancements are delivered across both the Cloud Private Edition and the on-premise SAP S/4HANA deployments.

Release Planning Our overarching release strategy for 2023–2025 remains unchanged. This strategy is ultimately designed to help customers maximize their system's potential while keeping them informed about the latest technological leaps.

 Important links: 

  • 3659273 – Restriction Note
  • 3493301 – Release Information Note
  • Release and maintenance strategy
  • Maintenance strategy for SAP S/4HANA

Before we get into the details, I’ve put together a quick executive summary slide below. This gives you a bird's-eye view of the most impactful innovations across all our key Lines of Business for this FPS1 release.

 In the sections that follow, I will walk you through my curated highlights across our Lines of Business—starting with Finance and moving through Sales, Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Localizations, and beyond.

Let's dive in!

Finance

AI-Powered Innovations (AI capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition only)

Cash Management Agent*

The Cash Management Agent empowers treasury professionals by utilizing Joule to interact seamlessly with cash and liquidity data. Users can check if all due bank statements are successfully received and processed, gather opening balances, and consider expected cash flows. The agent can calculate expected closing balances, identify potential cash shortages and surpluses, derive bank transfer proposals, and provide comprehensive end-of-day summaries. By leveraging this agent, users save a significant amount of time getting to correct cash positions and can start utilizing cash in the most efficient way possible.

Learn more by accessing the SAP Help Portal.

 

* This AI offering is currently in Beta and available at no cost. Pricing will be communicated once the AI offering becomes generally available. A separate subscription is required for use or testing. Learn more.

AI-assisted accrual object creation

AI-assisted manual accrual object creation streamlines the entity close process through the Joule copilot. It enables users to ask for accruals by scenario using natural-language input, and Joule automatically generates a proposed accrual object—including category, amount, life term, and GL account—using historical patterns. Users can edit these proposed attributes, and Joule adapts future proposals based on these edits. This functionality reduces the time needed to prepare manual accruals, lowers variance, ensures greater consistency, and significantly reduces missed or misstated accruals, empowering business users while improving proposal accuracy over time. 

 

AI-assisted fixed asset key figures explanation

This innovation introduces AI-assisted text in natural language that explains fixed asset key figures. It provides the ability to explain the origin of displayed values and exactly how fixed asset values are calculated—for example, illustrating in detail how the depreciation value results after midyear acquisitions when a specific depreciation key is used. This leads to an impressive 75% reduction in the effort required for analyzing fixed asset values and answering related questions, ultimately reducing the risk of noncompliance. NOTE: This AI feature is currently only available for customers with the activated business function "Universal Parallel Accounting" (FINS_PARALLEL_ACCOUNTING_BF).

 

AI-assisted settlement rule proposal for asset capitalization

The AI-assisted settlement rule proposal streamlines the capitalization process by proposing settlement rules for investment measures (projects or internal orders), as well as those not linked to an Asset under Construction (AuC) that still settle to a fixed asset. It proposes rules for full settlement, where receivers are always fixed assets, and allows users to easily edit proposals with percentages and receivers. This reduces the manual effort needed to create full settlement rules for investment measures and improves the accuracy of the asset class determination used for final assets. NOTE: There is no automatic asset master data creation yet. The functionality is only available for customers without the activated business function "Universal Parallel Accounting" (FINS_PARALLEL_ACCOUNTING_BF).

 

Please note that certain Joule features described in this blog may not yet be available in your system and are planned for release as part of the upcoming Joule release cycle scheduled for April 2026.

Core functional innovations

Transparency in intercompany processes using Financial Chains

This feature allows organizations to define commercial chains to structure intercompany business, supporting buy or sell relationships to address tax regulations, legal requirements, and group accounting principles. It simplifies master data for value flows by connecting multiple entities in commercial chains for intercompany logistics. This provides a strong foundation for future innovations in inventory value flows and delivers essential transparency into stock ownership across an intercompany commercial chain. NOTE: You need to activate business function VALUE_CHAIN_ANALYSIS to use the Financial Chains process.

Integrating results analysis with optimized contract management and inbound processing in revenue accounting

This integration smooths the connection between results analysis and optimized revenue accounting and reporting. It allows WBS elements to function as billing elements, assigns WBS elements to sales order items, and utilizes the cost-based percentage of completion method. This significantly improves financial transparency by accurately reflecting the financial state of ongoing projects and helps ensure that revenue recognition perfectly aligns with actual project completion.

Cost settlement for real estate scenarios, such as automated cost classification (Private Cloud)

This introduces a new cost-settlement process to allocate costs to participating real estate objects using a new master data object called "service group". Customers can set up their settlement hierarchy based on architecture objects, usable and rentable objects, and contract lifecycle management (CLM) contracts. By classifying incoming invoices and journal entry items to prepare for cost settlement, organizations can vastly increase efficiency and enhance the experience for users running real estate and contract management processes.

Central overhead cost planning and central price determination

This innovation ensures ongoing replication for activity types and actual activity rate replication from the central finance system directly to the source systems. This creates a holistic overview of planned costs by collecting all planned costs from various source systems. It results in a much more accurate activity rate calculation by incorporating all collected costs across system boundaries, allowing centrally calculated activity rates to be seamlessly replicated back to the source systems.

Copy functionality for interest rate swaps

Users can now copy existing interest-rate-swap transactions using new functionality that mirrors the experience in other SAP treasury and risk management solutions. This allows treasury teams to save considerable time and effort by taking advantage of prefilled fields when replicating existing interest-rate-swap transactions.

FX exposure management as an additional data source in the hedge management cockpit

This feature enables users to intuitively administer foreign exchange (FX) exposures tailored specifically to hedge-management use cases. Users can maintain FX exposures manually using the new SAP Fiori app "Manage FX Exposures" or load them via file upload functionality. By introducing FX exposure management as an additional data source in the Hedge Management Cockpit, teams can maintain exposures through a guided procedure, leverage a dedicated authorization concept, and make confident hedging decisions based on reliable source data.

Expanded usability in optimized contract management for revenue accounting

This update delivers broad improvements to revenue accounting capabilities to enhance usability, transparency, and alignment with modern UI standards. It includes extensions to the contract viewer report to display revenue accounting items and conditions, enhancements to the "Manage Revenue Contracts" app for creating time-based performance obligations, and a modernized "Manual Fulfillment" app upgraded to SAP Fiori with RAP. These updates ensure a more modern, consistent user interface while optimizing the handling and display of profitability segments.

Greater flexibility in contract combinations and performance obligation reassignments

This innovation enhances the functionality for contract combination, performance obligation reassignment, and contract splitting. By effectively processing suspended performance obligations, it provides users with greater flexibility in revenue contract lifecycle management, adapting more easily to evolving business agreements.

Accounting indicator for service with item-based accounting

The system now supports using an accounting indicator to differentiate actual costs and expenses when they are posted in the context of service in SAP S/4HANA with item-based accounting and event-based revenue recognition. This helps organizations understand and increase the profitability of contracts and services, improving service margins by providing clear insights into cost and revenue drivers while automating revenue recognition for service management.

New revenue recognition methods for execution order items with itemized billing

This feature expands revenue recognition by supporting the completed contract method for execution order items with itemized billing (both with and without planned revenue), as well as the revenue-based PoC method. This further automates and refines revenue recognition specifically for service management scenarios.

Revenue recognition for execution order items with resource-related cost items

Service management is further enhanced by supporting completed contract, cost-based PoC, and revenue-based PoC revenue recognition methods for execution order items with itemized billing using planned revenue based on Dynamic Item Processor-based (DIP-based) revenue lines. This drives higher automation in revenue recognition for complex service processes.

Support for individual settlement of maintenance orders

Users can now run settlements on maintenance orders where the order is part of a service hierarchy and the root element is subject to event-based revenue recognition (EBRR). This ensures accurate financial tracking and continues to automate revenue recognition for service management.

Event-based revenue recognition for sales orders with milestone billing (private cloud)

This update allows the use of sales orders with a milestone billing plan for sell-from-stock and third-party drop shipment scenarios, perfectly combined with event-based revenue recognition based on the completed contract method. This dramatically streamlines milestone billing processes and ensures accurate revenue tracking.

Enhancing the Manage Allocations and Manage Allocation Changes apps with improved definition of cycles and segments

This enhancement to the Manage Allocations app enables a more robust definition of allocation cycles and segments. Additionally, the introduction of the Manage Allocation Changes app allows users to seamlessly trace changes made to the definitions of these cycles and segments, supporting commonly used business processes within modern, intuitive user interfaces.

Support for early termination of performance obligations

Revenue accounting teams can now terminate individual performance obligations directly within a revenue contract. This provides crucial flexibility and supports customers in strictly meeting international accounting regulations.

Including joint venture accounting production month in universal allocation

This enhanced solution is designed for oil and gas companies that frequently use the production month to reflect actual usage dates. It captures journal entries with respect to the production month, allowing universal allocation to select costs for assignment through the production month rather than the posting period. This enables JVA customers to execute universal allocation with proper production month support for highly accurate JVA partner reporting.

App for managing closing run results for advanced foreign currency valuations

A new SAP Fiori app allows users to display and analyze the run results of advanced foreign currency valuations. It features an overview dashboard showing high-level statuses (Complete, Partially Complete, Failed, Running) and allows users to drill down into specific runs to understand valuation postings and highlight errors with root-cause messages. This provides a superior user experience through a centralized single source of truth, enabling end users to investigate root causes and take corrective action much faster.

*For a comprehensive list of all new features, technical details, and specific release notes for this LOB, explore the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 What's New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal. and filter by the line of business to see the full scope of innovations!

Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM)

AI-Powered Innovations (AI capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition only)

Joule for invoicing specialists to enable more-seamless navigation and transactional actions within billing documents

This innovation increases usability for the Joule copilot by providing intuitive navigational content to convergent invoicing SAP Fiori apps. Furthermore, it supports transactional content directly for convergent invoicing billing documents. By enabling these seamless conversational interactions, businesses can significantly reduce manual tasks and effort, increasing overall operational efficiency. Ultimately, this leads to an elevated customer experience and satisfaction, while actively helping to decrease days sales outstanding.

 

Additional machine learning features and enrichment of SAP Fiori apps for the private edition for behavioral insights

This release introduces time horizon improvements to machine learning features within the event and feature catalog, alongside improved usability in SAP Fiori apps that now feature additional filters, range buckets, and intent-based navigation (drill-down). AI-assisted behavioral insights for contract accounting analyzes historical customer behavior to accurately predict and explain payment risk, with results seamlessly integrated into your dunning and collections functionality. This empowers organizations to maximize collections success, increase cash flow by collecting sooner, and deeply understand the unique nature of each customer's journey. Teams can deploy these machine learning scenarios quickly and with lower risk by keeping data in place, requiring absolutely no data extraction or replication.

 

Joule for Internal Sales Rep - Subscription Contract Change Management with TCV impact overview

This feature enables advanced capabilities for subscription contract management, providing internal sales representatives with a clear overview of the impact of change processes on Total Contract Value (TCV). It allows for manual human validation of the financial impact on the customer contract before proceeding with any changes. By empowering representatives with a simplified change process based on conversational Joule, users receive accurate details on pricing benefits before confirming modifications. This natural language chatbot interaction dramatically improves user experience, satisfaction, and efficiency, while also highlighting better cross-sell opportunities and deals.

Core functional innovations

Extension of Scope Item for Subscription Order Management (7WB) to support sales item and change processes

This update provides the organizational structure and master data setup required for the creation of a subscription order. It allows internal sales representatives to create a subscription order with subscription products and multilevel AVC subscription products to create a subscription contract. Furthermore, it enables the transfer of the subscription contract to the follow-up system, such as Convergent Invoicing, via the order distribution interface. This replication triggers a billing plan and generates recurring and one-time charge BITs in SAP CI for the contract. Users can leverage the subscription contract lifecycle app to display the new subscription contract , and can now also execute change processes directly on the subscription contract. By leveraging this ready-to-run content for subscription order creation scenarios, organizations can reduce implementation time, minimize customization, and support faster time-to-value while promoting consistent and scalable deployment of subscription-based business models.

Ability to maintain payment card data during subscription order entry processing

This feature allows for the management of customer payment card data directly during order entry instead of relying on master data. Users can now register payment cards directly at the header and item level of a subscription order during order creation. The payment cards registered for a specific transaction are not registered for the business partner associated with the subscription order, which means that individual payment cards can be associated with each specific item. This functionality significantly enhances flexibility and transparency in subscription order entry processing.

SALES

AI-Powered Innovations (AI capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition only)

AI-assisted detecting of expiring prices using Joule

This AI feature helps pricing specialists detect prices that will expire within a specified time frame using the Joule copilot. The role-based assistant can fetch and display expiring prices and condition records based on a given time frame, extend the validity or amounts of condition records, and perform mass changes while displaying progress and a log file of the changes. By automating follow-up activities like prolongation or handling expiring prices, this innovation significantly reduces the time and cost associated with detecting expiring prices and creating new ones. It removes repetitive, time-consuming tasks and reduces human-prone manual errors, ultimately increasing the efficiency and work quality of pricing specialists.

 AI-assisted fetching and listing of open sales documents

This feature enables internal sales representatives to effortlessly fetch a list of open sales documents using Joule. Joule displays open sales orders based on available sales document data—such as sales order type, sold-to party, document date, material, customer reference, and creation date—as well as organizational data like sales organization, distribution channel, division, and sales office. By allowing users to interactively fetch this list through a conversational assistant, it significantly increases the efficiency of internal sales reps by reducing manual effort.

 AI-assisted Sales Quotation Changes using Joule

This innovation enables AI-assisted changes to sales quotations directly through the Joule copilot. It provides users with the capability to easily update line item quantities, modify the price of a line item via condition type, and adjust critical dates such as the requested delivery date, quote valid-to date, and customer reference date. Additionally, it allows users to update delivery plants or set reasons for rejection. Enabling these sales quotation changes through Joule increases the efficiency of internal sales representatives by substantially reducing manual data entry and effort

Core functional innovations

Multistage (Multilevel) intercompany sales*

This release introduces a new business process for multistage intercompany sales, enabling two or more affiliated companies running in the same system to participate in a single intercompany process. The selling company can sell a product from a stock owned by an affiliated company, buying the goods directly from the delivering affiliate or intermediate companies. This involves drop shipments where goods are directly delivered from the stocked plant to the customer, utilizing valuated stock in transit for a smooth change of control. Organizations gain a comprehensive, highly automated process that is deeply embedded into product compliance, trade compliance, transport management, revenue recognition, profitability reporting, product costing, and group reporting. This consistently applies changes in customer-facing sales orders throughout the end-to-end document flow while providing a robust process monitor for issue detection.

*Additional  license/premium SKU maybe required for this feature. 

Configuration of role-based access to price and cost details in sales quotations and sales contracts

Organizations can now configure exactly which user roles can access specific price and cost details of a pricing procedure within sales inquiries, sales quotations, sales contracts, and scheduling agreements. The system controls access based on your configuration of access levels (No access, Display access, or Change access). This restriction capability is highly beneficial when sensitive information, such as profit margins, should stay hidden for certain users or when editing specific price elements needs to be limited, helping to secure sensitive price and cost information across your sales documents.

New sales order triggered by a customer returns order

Customer returns processing has been enhanced to offer customers a discount on a new sales order during a return where they are not entitled to a credit note (such as when the product guarantee has expired). The system can now automatically trigger the creation of a new sales document—which can be either a Sales Order or a Delivery Free of Charge—based directly on the return order. This allows businesses to manage returns orders more efficiently, significantly simplify follow-up processing, and increase transparency during complex returns scenarios.

OData V4 API for returns orders

Users can now manage returns orders more efficiently using the Customer Return OData V4 API to create, read, update, and delete returns orders from external systems. This includes operations on basic entities such as the returns order header, items, pricing elements, partner entities, and text. By leveraging the new features offered in the ABAP RESTful application programming model framework and the latest OData V4 protocol, this API utilizes a lightweight JSON format that improves processing time and reduces resource consumption for both clients and servers. Users also gain the flexibility to process requests in either synchronous or asynchronous mode.

 Sales contract OData API new version

This update introduces an OData version 4 service that enables users to create, read, update, and delete single entities (such as items) in sales contracts directly from external systems. This drastically improves holistic contract management and ensures a future-proof, stable architecture that is consistent across objects using the RAP framework. Organizations benefit from improved processing times and reduced resource consumption thanks to a lightweight JSON format. The framework also allows for minimal responses containing only the keys of created entities, direct filtering of subentities, and requires no additional effort to set up a communication arrangement, as it is directly included in existing configurations.

Sales Order OData v4 API support of sales orders with configurable items

The modern Sales Order OData v4 API has been enhanced to support the creation of sales order items with Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC). This improves holistic sales order management by enabling the seamless integration of sales orders containing variant configuration with external systems. Built on the ABAP RESTful application programming model framework, it assures a future-ready and stable architecture. Just like the sales contract API, it allows users to choose between synchronous or asynchronous modes, request minimal responses to improve performance, directly filter subentities, and operates without any additional communication arrangement effort.

Navigation to NAST output control in the Manage Sales Quotations - Version 2 and Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 apps

This update allows navigation to new application server technology (NAST) output control directly within SAP Fiori apps for sales. Users can now seamlessly view a PDF version of the output document for order confirmation printing in the "Manage Sales Quotations - Version 2" app, and for invoice printing in the "Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2" app. This enhances overall operational efficiency by providing single-point access to critical data, which in turn reduces time and effort and accelerates processing times.

Usage of business rules to determine default item categories in sales documents

Organizations can now embed flexible business rules in advanced field control using Business Rule Framework plus (BRFplus) to automatically determine the default item category for sales orders, sales quotations, and sales orders without charge. This feature provides a standard BRFplus application template, enabling customers to select predefined criteria to build decision tables and efficiently define their own applications. This provides a highly flexible solution for determining default item categories and increases efficiency during setup by simply copying the standard template.

For a comprehensive list of all new features, technical details, and specific release notes for this LOB, explore the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 What's New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal. and filter by the line of business to see the full scope of innovations!

 Supply Chain

Core functional innovations

Ability to consider product allocations during an availability check for sales quotations

This feature enables the use of the characteristic catalog for sales documents directly within sales quotations, allowing users to activate a product allocation object and create planning data used by quotations. By checking maintained product allocations during an availability check for the sales quotation, and considering these reservations during the check for a subsequent sales order referencing the quotation, organizations significantly increase their order planning and fulfillment capabilities.

 Support of sales quotations in supply protection

Users now have the option to define supply protection for quotations based on the characteristics catalog for sales documents. This provides an option to set up protection that is not only considered as a restriction from the perspective of a sales quotation but also acts as active protection, thereby increasing the flexibility and reliability of supply protection.

Cut-off times for product availability check

In the advanced ATP product availability check (PAC), supply elements planned to arrive after a certain time of the day can now be moved directly to the beginning of the next day using fine-granular configuration levels (including plant, document purpose, and ATP category). This improves the balance between a progressive and a conservative ATP check, thereby substantially increasing the accuracy of ATP confirmations.

Interactive backorder processing (iBOP): Support for rounding and additional features

This update enables support for quantity rounding during availability checks within the interactive backorder processing (iBOP) application, allowing users to use delivery proposal results when performing manual confirmation adjustments. It also introduces a monitoring feature for requirements and order items that underwent unrestricted checks during previous runs. This prevents shipping bottlenecks by ensuring confirmed quantities align with packaging constraints, enhances decision-making by showing the actual system situation, and tracks unrestricted check history to facilitate faster troubleshooting.

Plant determination in master data substitution

Organizations gain highly configurable behavior for plant substitution during the advanced availability check. While the classic behavior determines alternative plants based on the requested plant, a new option allows the system to determine a list of alternative plants independent of the originally requested plant, either including or excluding it. This new substitution type dramatically eases the maintenance of plant substitution master data and provides the flexibility to optionally ignore the originally entered plant during the advanced ATP check.

Providing analytics for safety stock levels with embedded SAP Fiori app

A new SAP Fiori analytical app delivers enhanced safety stock analysis, offering real-time visibility into safety stock levels across products and locations. It features flexible filtering, displays both quantity and financial values, provides insight into key safety stock drivers (demand, supply, service variability), and allows drill-down capabilities with data export options. This provides real-time, actionable insights to facilitate data-driven network-wide decision-making, increases planner productivity, and helps align safety stock levels with demand forecasts while addressing root causes of variability. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

 

 

 

Cost optimization in safety stock calculation

This feature enables cost optimization in safety stock calculation by allowing users to maintain stockholding costs, handling costs, and transportation costs for products. The system calculates safety stock for products at each network location while finding the optimal balance between these associated costs. Planners get a cost-optimized result for Target Service Levels and safety stock amounts, lowering the total costs for stockholding and distribution while ensuring customer orders are fulfilled with minimal costs. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Dealer product copying and deletion

Users now have options in the SAP Fiori apps (Manage Dealer Product and Application Jobs - eSPP) to copy single or multiple dealer products from one dealer location to another, as well as new reports and job templates to mark and delete outdated products. This empowers users to create new dealer products quickly and consistently while improving data quality by keeping only the necessary master data. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Mass maintenance for dealer products

This introduces a new data type to create and maintain dealer products in the SAP Fiori app "Manage Mass Maintenance - eSPP Data," supporting both active and simulation version updates. Additionally, dealer products are added as a data type for file upload using the "Manage Mass Processing - eSPP Data" app. This reduces the effort and time needed to maintain master data and provides a consistent, central framework for mass maintenance. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Mass maintenance for product group types and product groups

Similar to dealer products, a new data type is added to maintain product group types and product group assignments within the "Manage Mass Maintenance" and "Manage Mass Processing" SAP Fiori apps. This greatly reduces the manual effort needed to maintain these assignments while offering a centralized mass maintenance framework. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Simplified maintenance of bill of distributions (BODs)

This provides a modernized user experience based on SAP Fiori design for the maintenance of extended service parts planning (eSPP) bills of distributions (BODs). It includes CSV upload/download capabilities, mass creation options to input multiple child locations, and a search utility to find specific locations quickly in large BODs. This aligns with the strategic UX direction of SAP applications, improving navigation, reducing clicks, and simplifying the maintenance of the bill of distribution structure. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Ability to use complex selections with product group types and product groups in service parts planning processes

A new SAP Fiori app allows users to define, alter, and delete complex selections combining product group types and product groups using AND/OR logic. Users can simulate results to see matching products and use these combinations directly in the planning service manager (PSM) framework. This adds immense flexibility, saves handling effort for defining specific groups, and improves overall planning quality by considering product-specific segmentation. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

Intermittent time series forecast model

This feature leverages the Intermittent Time Series Forecasting (ITSF) algorithm of the predictive analytics library (PAL) to generate forecasts for products with intermittent, hard-to-predict demand. It uses automatic model selection to determine if ITSF is the best fit and visualizes the results graphically and tabularly, vastly improving forecast quality for spare parts in upcoming periods. NOTE: This road map item also applies to SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA.

For a comprehensive list of all new features, technical details, and specific release notes for this LOB, explore the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 What's New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal. and filter by the line of business to see the full scope of innovations!

Warehouse Management

AI-Powered Innovations (AI capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition only)

AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning, feature enrichment

This enhancement expands planned workload and forecast capabilities to the outbound staging process while refining the forecast UI to provide functional drill-downs.
